name: align_exceeds_input
reason: Aligning down should never produce a value larger than the original input
output_tail:

name: result_not_multiple
reason: The core contract of alignment is that the result is a multiple of b; violating this means alignment is broken
output_tail:
ltiple(a: usize, b: usize)
    |       ------------------------------------------------ at the end of the function body
...
509 |         align_to_usize(a, b) % b != 0,
    |         ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ failed this postcondition

name: not_idempotent
reason: Aligning an already-aligned value again must be a no-op; failure means the function is not a true projection
output_tail:
------------------- at the end of the function body
...
509 |         align_to_usize(align_to_usize(a, b), b) != align_to_usize(a, b),
    |         ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ failed this postcondition

error: aborting due to 1 previous error; 1 warning emitted

name: already_aligned_changes
reason: When the input is already a multiple of b the result must equal the input; any deviation is a bug
output_tail:
ed_changes(a: usize, b: usize)

...
509 |         align_to_usize(a, b) != a,
    |         ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ failed this postcondition

name: gap_too_large
reason: The distance between the input and its aligned value must be strictly less than b; a gap >= b means a closer aligned address was skipped
output_tail:
ap_too_large(a: usize, b: usize)
    |       ------------------------------------------ at the end of the function body
...
509 |         a - align_to_usize(a, b) >= b,
    |         ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ failed this postcondition
